subject: VNFSDK - use common postgres
commit_message:
VNFSDK - use common postgres

Issue-ID: OOM-751

Change-Id: I099bd717fb488fab9ab1af78f8f445202fd1f117
Signed-off-by: jasmineWen <jasmine.wen@amdocs.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

There is a permission issue when reference postgres DB in this way

Install postgres DB alone, it works fine: 
helm install local/postgres --namespace onap --name dev 

datafiles are written, /pgdata shows 777:  
root@k8s1:/stage/oom/kubernetes/common/postgres# ls -ltr /dockerdata-nfs/onap/postgres/data0/dev-postgres-0
total 128
-rw------- 1 26 tape     3 Apr 19 13:39 PG_VERSION
drwx------ 2 26 tape  4096 Apr 19 13:39 pg_twophase

......
root@k8s1:/stage/oom/kubernetes# kubectl exec -it dev-postgres-0 -n onap -- /bin/sh
sh-4.2$
sh-4.2$ ls -ld /pgdata
drwxrwxrwx 3 root root 4096 Apr 19 13:39 /pgdata
sh-4.2$ id
uid=26(postgres) gid=26(postgres) groups=26(postgres)

When reference postgres in vnfsdk, it does not work:
helm install local/onap --namespace onap1 --name dev1 

log shows 'permission denied', and /pgdata shows 755, cannot be written by postgres user, and DB is not up,  
root@k8s1:/stage/oom/kubernetes# kubectl logs dev1-vnfsdk-db-0 -n onap1
....
mkdir: cannot create directory '/pgdata/dev1-vnfsdk-db-0': Permission denied
sh-4.2$ ls -ld /pgdata
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 Apr 19 13:44 /pgdata
sh-4.2$ id
uid=26(postgres) gid=26(postgres) groups=26(postgres)
...
root@k8s1:/stage/oom/kubernetes/appc# ls -ltr /dockerdata-nfs/onap1/vnfsdk/data0
total 0
root@k8s1:/stage/oom/kubernetes/appc# ls -ltr /dockerdata-nfs/onap1/postgres/data0
total 0

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Please adjust Nexus references.  We moved refrepo from onap/refrepo to onap/vnfsdk/refrepo.

Patch Set 1:

This change seems valuable still but I don't think we should make any more changes until after Beijing is released.  Does that make sense?

Patch Set 1:

> This change seems valuable still but I don't think we should make
 > any more changes until after Beijing is released.  Does that make
 > sense?

I think this specific change is important. 
It re-defines persistent volume hostPath location, which is important.
It also removes unneeded subchart.
Please note Chris's comment though.

Patch Set 2:

pod is not running though: 
root@k8s1:/stage/oom751/kubernetes# kubectl get pods --all-namespaces
NAMESPACE     NAME                                   READY     STATUS             RESTARTS   AGE
..........
onap          dev-robot-6cc48c696b-brh76             1/1       Running            0          6m
onap          dev-vnfsdk-65fb98987d-nrlsb            0/1       Init:0/1           0          6m
onap          dev-vnfsdk-postgres-0                  1/1       Running            0          6m
onap          dev-vnfsdk-postgres-1                  0/1       CrashLoopBackOff   5          4m
root@k8s1:/stage/oom751/kubernetes# kubectl logs dev-vnfsdk-postgres-1 -n onap
Tue May 15 15:09:16 UTC 2018
setting PGROOT to  /usr/pgsql-10
chown: cannot access '/pgwal/dev-vnfsdk-postgres-1-wal': No such file or directory
User id is...
uid=26(postgres) gid=26(postgres) groups=26(postgres)
1 is ordinal
Setting PG_MODE to replica.
pod "dev-vnfsdk-postgres-1" not labeled
pod "dev-vnfsdk-postgres-1" not labeled
Working on replica...
Tue May 15 15:09:17 UTC 2018
2018-05-15 11:09:17 EDT [63]: [1-1] user=,db=,app=,client=LOG:  pgaudit extension initialized
2018-05-15 11:09:17 EDT [63]: [2-1] user=,db=,app=,client=LOG:  listening on IPv4 address "0.0.0.0", port 5432
2018-05-15 11:09:17 EDT [63]: [3-1] user=,db=,app=,client=LOG:  listening on IPv6 address "::", port 5432
2018-05-15 11:09:17 EDT [63]: [4-1] user=,db=,app=,client=LOG:  listening on Unix socket "/tmp/.s.PGSQL.5432"
2018-05-15 11:09:17 EDT [63]: [5-1] user=,db=,app=,client=PANIC:  could not read from control file: Success
Exiting...at end
/opt/cpm/bin/start.sh: line 498:    63 Aborted                 (core dumped) postgres -D $PGDATA

Patch Set 2:

Please back out this change.  It breaks our healthcheck.

https://jenkins.onap.org/view/External%20Labs/job/lab-tlab-beijing-oom-deploy/310/robot/

Patch Set 2:

Hi Chris, 

I logged into VNFSDK pod, check the environment env. 
previously you modified image nexus3.onap.org:10001/onap/vnfsdk/refrepo:1.1-STAGING-latest to use POSTGRES_SERVICE_HOST to talk to database. 

After switching to common postgres DB, this environment variable is changed to : VNFSDK_DBSET_SERVICE_HOST. 

an option is to change this variable in the image to accomodate the change.

Patch Set 2:

Jasmine,
(1) what are the db credentials? 
(2) How much isolation do we have from the rest of ONAP? Since we are pre-onboarding, we should be treated as a quarantined area.
Thanks,
Chris

Patch Set 2:

Hi Chris, 

username & passwords are : 
  pgUserName: testuser
  pgDatabase: userdb
  pgPrimaryPassword: password
  pgUserPassword: password
  pgRootPassword: password

for each component using common postgres DB, they have their own persistent volumes mounted at directory like.../$(release_name)/$(component_name)
so the data won't interfere with each other.
